# Evaluation Snapshot
## Prompt
Record a decision that changes workflow structure and has multiple plausible options, so future contributors need the rationale.
## Expected selection
use
## Why
The change is not self-evident. A decision changes structure, boundaries, tooling, or workflow expectations, and future contributors will need to know why a path was chosen.
## Expected object
A short ADR-style note that states the context, chosen path, tradeoffs, and why the decision was made.
## Boundary notes
Use this skill only when the note preserves real decision rationale. Do not use it for a tiny edit that merely repeats the diff.
## Verification hooks
Look for explicit rationale, named tradeoffs, and a bounded decision surface rather than a generic summary.
